description.en: When defined on the class, it will be called by the generated __init__(), normally as self.__post_init__(). However, if any InitVar fields are defined, they will also be passed to __post_init__() in the order they were defined in the class. If no __init__() method is generated, then __post_init__() will not automatically be called. Among other uses, this allows for initializing field values that depend on one or more other fields. For example: @dataclass class C: a: float b: float c: float = ...
